infrastructure_finding.title: Parallel chrome-mcp workers share cookies — RBAC negative matrix cannot be run in parallel
infrastructure_finding.description: All worker tabs in the same Chrome browser profile share httpOnly JWT cookies for origin http://localhost:3000. When multiple workers log in as different roles in parallel, the last login wins and overrides all other sessions. Verified by calling /api/v1/auth/me from W7 tab (expected Saran V / Employee) and getting Fayaz M / SUPER_ADMIN response instead. This means role-specific RBAC worker shards (W7 Employee, and any similar) cannot run concurrently with higher-privilege workers; they produce false SUPER_ADMIN data leaks in every response.
infrastructure_finding.recommendation: Run RBAC negative matrix tests serially, or use separate Chrome profiles / incognito windows per worker, or drop cookies-based auth in favor of Authorization header per-request for test mode.

rbac_matrix.note: Redirected to /dashboard on first attempt (looked like PASS), but /api/v1/auth/me returned SUPER_ADMIN role (cookie overridden by parallel worker). Cannot distinguish role enforcement from cookie hijack.

pages.note: Demo account card for Saran V (Employee) visible and clickable. POST /api/v1/auth/login returned 200 on login.

Immediately after Saran V login, sidebar showed Employee-appropriate items only: HOME, MY SPACE (Dashboard/Profile/Payslips/Attendance/Leaves/Documents), PEOPLE (Departments/Approvals), HR OPERATIONS (Shift/Leave/Contracts), PROJECTS & WORK (Calendar), ADMIN (Workflows). No Employees list, no Payroll, no System Admin. After ~30s, parallel worker cookie override caused sidebar to expand to full admin menu (Employees, Payroll, System Admin, Organization Setup, etc.).

summary: Login worked. First-navigation /employees redirected back to /dashboard (apparent PASS) but within seconds, shared-cookie override from parallel workers replaced Saran V's session with SUPER_ADMIN. /api/v1/auth/me confirmed identity flip to Fayaz M. RBAC negative matrix cannot be validly executed under the current parallel worker design — flagged as infrastructure issue BUG-W7-01.
